query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, questionText, questionType, options, correctAnswer, `order`) VALUES
-- Lesson 1: Biblical Theology of Preaching (360091)
(360091, 'What is the primary purpose of biblical preaching?', 'multiple_choice', '["Entertainment","Information transfer","Proclamation of God''s Word for transformation","Academic lecture"]', 'Proclamation of God''s Word for transformation', 1),
(360091, 'According to 2 Timothy 4:2, preachers are commanded to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Preach the Word","Tell stories","Share opinions","Debate theology"]', 'Preach the Word', 2),
(360091, 'The Greek word "kerygma" refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Teaching","Proclamation or preaching","Singing","Prayer"]', 'Proclamation or preaching', 3),
(360091, 'Which Old Testament figure is often considered a model for prophetic preaching?', 'multiple_choice', '["David","Solomon","Moses","Isaiah"]', 'Moses', 4),
(360091, 'The authority of preaching comes from:', 'multiple_choice', '["The preacher''s education","The congregation''s approval","The Word of God","Cultural relevance"]', 'The Word of God', 5),
(360091, 'What distinguishes Christian preaching from other forms of public speaking?', 'multiple_choice', '["Better techniques","Divine commission and biblical content","Larger audiences","Professional training"]', 'Divine commission and biblical content', 6),
(360091, 'According to Romans 10:14-17, faith comes through:', 'multiple_choice', '["Signs and wonders","Hearing the Word","Personal experience","Philosophical argument"]', 'Hearing the Word', 7),
(360091, 'The New Testament emphasizes that preaching should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Entertaining","Christ-centered","Politically relevant","Philosophically sophisticated"]', 'Christ-centered', 8),
(360091, 'Which apostle wrote extensively about the theology of preaching?', 'multiple_choice', '["Peter","James","John","Paul"]', 'Paul', 9),
(360091, 'The ultimate goal of biblical preaching is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Church growth","God''s glory and human transformation","Denominational loyalty","Cultural influence"]', 'God''s glory and human transformation', 10),
(360091, 'Explain the relationship between the authority of Scripture and the authority of preaching.', 'short_answer', NULL, NULL, 11),

-- Lesson 2: Expository Preaching (360092)
(360092, 'Expository preaching is best defined as:', 'multiple_choice', '["Preaching that exposes sin","Preaching that explains and applies a biblical text","Preaching without notes","Preaching topically"]', 'Preaching that explains and applies a biblical text', 1),
(360092, 'The primary text for expository preaching should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Current events","A biblical passage","Popular books","Personal experiences"]', 'A biblical passage', 2),
(360092, 'What is the main advantage of expository preaching?', 'multiple_choice', '["It''s easier to prepare","It ensures biblical authority drives the message","It''s more entertaining","It requires less study"]', 'It ensures biblical authority drives the message', 3),
(360092, 'The "big idea" in expository preaching refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["The longest point","The central theme of the text","The introduction","The illustration"]', 'The central theme of the text', 4),
(360092, 'Exegesis in sermon preparation means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Reading into the text","Drawing meaning out of the text","Ignoring context","Focusing on application only"]', 'Drawing meaning out of the text', 5),
(360092, 'Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of expository preaching?', 'multiple_choice', '["Text-driven","Contextually faithful","Ignores original meaning","Applicational"]', 'Ignores original meaning', 6),
(360092, 'The structure of an expository sermon should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Follow a predetermined outline","Emerge from the text itself","Always have three points","Ignore the text''s structure"]', 'Emerge from the text itself', 7),
(360092, 'Haddon Robinson is known for his emphasis on:', 'multiple_choice', '["Topical preaching","The big idea in preaching","Narrative preaching","Liturgical preaching"]', 'The big idea in preaching', 8),
(360092, 'Application in expository preaching should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Be avoided","Flow naturally from the text","Be added artificially","Replace exposition"]', 'Flow naturally from the text', 9),
(360092, 'The goal of expository preaching is to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Display the preacher''s knowledge","Let the text speak its message to contemporary hearers","Entertain the congregation","Fulfill a time requirement"]', 'Let the text speak its message to contemporary hearers', 10),
(360092, 'Describe the process of moving from text to sermon in expository preaching.', 'short_answer', NULL, NULL, 11),

-- Lesson 3: Sermon Preparation (360093)
(360093, 'The first step in sermon preparation should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Writing the introduction","Prayer and text selection","Finding illustrations","Outlining points"]', 'Prayer and text selection', 1),
(360093, 'How much time should typically be devoted to sermon preparation?', 'multiple_choice', '["1 hour","1 hour per minute of preaching","As little as possible","Only on Saturday night"]', '1 hour per minute of preaching', 2),
(360093, 'The study of the original languages helps the preacher to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Impress the congregation","Understand the text more accurately","Avoid application","Preach longer sermons"]', 'Understand the text more accurately', 3),
(360093, 'Historical-grammatical interpretation involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignoring history","Understanding the text in its original context","Allegorizing everything","Focusing only on grammar"]', 'Understanding the text in its original context', 4),
(360093, 'Consulting commentaries should occur:', 'multiple_choice', '["Before personal study","After personal study of the text","Never","Only for difficult passages"]', 'After personal study of the text', 5),
(360093, 'The sermon outline should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Be memorized word for word","Serve the communication of the text''s message","Always have exactly three points","Be identical for every sermon"]', 'Serve the communication of the text''s message', 6),
(360093, 'What is the purpose of a sermon introduction?', 'multiple_choice', '["To fill time","To engage listeners and introduce the subject","To tell jokes","To review last week"]', 'To engage listeners and introduce the subject', 7),
(360093, 'Transitions in a sermon help to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Confuse the audience","Guide listeners through the message","Add length","Show off vocabulary"]', 'Guide listeners through the message', 8),
(360093, 'The conclusion of a sermon should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Introduce new material","Summarize and call for response","Simply stop","Repeat the introduction"]', 'Summarize and call for response', 9),
(360093, 'Manuscript, outline, and note-free are all:', 'multiple_choice', '["Types of sermons","Delivery methods","Interpretation approaches","Application styles"]', 'Delivery methods', 10),
(360093, 'Outline your personal sermon preparation process from text selection to delivery.', 'short_answer', NULL, NULL, 11),

-- Lesson 4: Sermon Structure (360094)
(360094, 'A well-structured sermon helps listeners to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Fall asleep","Follow and remember the message","Feel confused","Lose interest"]', 'Follow and remember the message', 1),
(360094, 'The "hook" in a sermon refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["A fishing illustration","An attention-getting opening","The conclusion","A transition"]', 'An attention-getting opening', 2),
(360094, 'Deductive sermon structure moves from:', 'multiple_choice', '["Specific to general","General principle to specific application","Story to doctrine","Application to text"]', 'General principle to specific application', 3),
(360094, 'Inductive sermon structure moves from:', 'multiple_choice', '["General to specific","Specific observations to general conclusion","Conclusion to introduction","Application to text"]', 'Specific observations to general conclusion', 4),
(360094, 'The body of the sermon should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Be as long as possible","Develop the main idea with supporting points","Avoid the biblical text","Focus only on stories"]', 'Develop the main idea with supporting points', 5),
(360094, 'Parallel structure in sermon points means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Points that contradict each other","Points with similar grammatical form","Points of equal length","Points that repeat"]', 'Points with similar grammatical form', 6),
(360094, 'How many main points should a sermon typically have?', 'multiple_choice', '["Exactly three always","As many as the text requires, usually 2-5","At least ten","Only one"]', 'As many as the text requires, usually 2-5', 7),
(360094, 'Sub-points in a sermon should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Stand alone","Support and develop the main points","Contradict main points","Be unrelated to the text"]', 'Support and develop the main points', 8),
(360094, 'Unity in sermon structure means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Everything relates to the central idea","All points are the same length","The sermon is short","There are no illustrations"]', 'Everything relates to the central idea', 9),
(360094, 'The purpose of sermon structure is to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Impress with organization","Serve clear communication of truth","Follow tradition","Fill time"]', 'Serve clear communication of truth', 10),
(360094, 'Compare and contrast deductive and inductive sermon structures with examples.', 'short_answer', NULL, NULL, 11),

-- Lesson 5: Illustrations and Application (360095)
(360095, 'The primary purpose of sermon illustrations is to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Entertain the audience","Clarify and apply biblical truth","Fill time","Show the preacher''s creativity"]', 'Clarify and apply biblical truth', 1),
(360095, 'Good illustrations should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Long and complex","Clear, relevant, and memorable","Always humorous","Unrelated to the point"]', 'Clear, relevant, and memorable', 2),
(360095, 'Sources for illustrations include:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only illustration books","Life, literature, history, and current events","Only personal stories","Only Bible stories"]', 'Life, literature, history, and current events', 3),
(360095, 'Application in preaching answers the question:', 'multiple_choice', '["What does this mean?","So what? How does this apply to life?","Who wrote this?","When was this written?"]', 'So what? How does this apply to life?', 4),
(360095, 'Application should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Vague and general","Specific and practical","Avoided entirely","Only for new believers"]', 'Specific and practical', 5),
(360095, 'The danger of too many illustrations is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Clarity","Obscuring the biblical text","Engagement","Memorability"]', 'Obscuring the biblical text', 6),
(360095, 'Personal illustrations should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Always make the preacher look good","Be used carefully and honestly","Be avoided entirely","Dominate every sermon"]', 'Be used carefully and honestly', 7),
(360095, 'Application should address:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only behavior","Head, heart, and hands (belief, attitude, action)","Only emotions","Only theology"]', 'Head, heart, and hands (belief, attitude, action)', 8),
(360095, 'When using illustrations from others'' lives:', 'multiple_choice', '["Always use real names","Protect privacy and get permission when needed","Make up details","Exaggerate for effect"]', 'Protect privacy and get permission when needed', 9),
(360095, 'The best applications flow from:', 'multiple_choice', '["The preacher''s preferences","The text''s own implications","Current trends","Denominational requirements"]', 'The text''s own implications', 10),
(360095, 'Explain how to develop effective applications that address different areas of life.', 'short_answer', NULL, NULL, 11);

logs: ERROR 1054 (42S22) at line 1: Unknown column 'questionText' in 'field list'
